vo_hub_shipmaster_ambient_b: {0.00->1.91}I'll need to requisition some more Hellpods.
vo_hub_shipmaster_ambient_c: {0.00->1.97}Can't forget to check up on shuttle maintenance.
vo_hub_shipmaster_arc1_beat1_mission_suc: {0.03->5.91}Maybe we don't even need the TCS. You Helldivers seem to kill plenty of bugs just fine on your own.
vo_hub_shipmaster_arc2_beat5_mission_suc: {0.04->0.82}Welcome back. {1.23->3.67}The Illuminate might be an advanced species, but {4.09->5.74}they're no match for the Helldivers.
vo_hub_shipmaster_arc2_beat6_mission_suc: {0.06->4.60}Mmm! Smells like squid guts in here. Must've been a good mission.
vo_hub_shipmaster_generic_reward_a: {0.00->2.93}In recognition of our contribution to the war effort.
vo_hub_shipmaster_generic_reward_c: {0.07->3.77}Super Earth High Command has graced us with a sign of their approval.
vo_hub_shipmaster_generic_reward_d: {0.00->1.79}Freedom is its own rewardâ{2.49->3.45}but this doesn't hurt.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc1_beat1_c: {0.26->3.26}Sometimes I forget that we're just one piece of the puzzle. {3.88->7.95}Case in point, once we push back the bugs, the SEAF will take over, {8.51->10.35}guarding the construction of the TCS. {11.13->12.52}Everyone has their part.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc1_beat3_a: {0.17->1.07}I was just thinking: {1.58->6.24}warping across the Federation like this would be impossible without Element 710. {7.11->9.44}Which comes from the bugs themselves. {10.02->14.60}Their own corpses are fueling the creation of more corpses...{16.87->18.30}something satisfying about that.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc1_beat3_b: {0.18->3.98}The engineers filled the TCS tanks with enough Termicide to last over a year. {4.46->7.54}Should be easy enough to replenish, once all the Terminids are dead.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc1_beat4_a: {0.25->2.74}The Automatons are a good reminder to never get complacent. {3.39->4.50}It's like I always say.{5.10->8.45}Military spending is always money well spent.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc1_beat9_pl: {0.06->4.38}I'd ask if you were going to take a break, but I already know the answer. {5.09->9.34}You and your fellow Helldivers inspire me every day.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat1_a: {0.04->3.71}We're advancing on Cyberstan, just like in the good old days.{4.27->10.74}It won't be easy. Unless we take on easy operations. But even then, they won't be as easy as they{10.74->12.32}would have been, otherwise.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat1_b: {0.00->2.27}No matter what they're hiding on Cyberstan,{2.66->3.95}we'll uncover it.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat1_c: {0.00->2.60}This is just like the good old days, right Helldiver?{2.98->7.72}Blazing a trail straight into the Den of Dissidence, like our forefathers, {7.96->10.29}armed only with the courage in our hearts{10.42->12.56}and the artillery at our command.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat1_d: {0.00->1.91}With the Bastion Tanks back in action,{2.27->5.58}we'll be just like the Helldivers from the first Galactic War, you and I.{6.15->7.83}We'll be unstoppable.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat2_a: {1.49->2.42}Cyberstan...{2.90->5.51}That sure is one ugly planet.{6.13->9.59}But we'll give it the cosmetic surgery of Freedom, won't we, Helldiver?
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat2_b: {0.04->4.55}So, the masterminds behind the Automatons reveal themselves at last.{4.55->8.29}The Cyborgs thought they could catch us by surprise, huh?{8.29->11.89}Well, we could smell their joint grease a galaxy away.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat2_c: {0.00->5.52}As if we didn't know the Cyborgs were behind it all! We should broadcast THAT to Cyberstan.{5.92->9.94}Can't have those oil-soiled dissidents thinking they got one over on us.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat3_c: {0.00->7.94}How dare they attack Super Earth. All those innocent families, all those prosperous businesses...
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat3_d: {0.00->5.15}Textbook tactics. Create a diversion, split our forces. Its a desperate attempt to keep us from{5.15->6.22}taking Cyberstan.{6.87->10.05}But that just means we'll crush them on two fronts at once.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat3_e: {0.00->2.17}They're going for both Cyberstan and Super Earth, huh?{3.10->7.34}Well, Liberty has two fists, and she's ambidextrous.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat4_a: {0.00->1.88}We've touched down on Cyberstan itself.{2.30->8.90}Our old enemy is waiting down there: not just the Cyborgs themselves, but their twisted socialist{8.90->9.50}agenda.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat4_b: {0.04->3.59}The Cyborgs have a perverse predisposition for violence.{4.00->5.33}If we're going to stamp them out,{5.94->7.82}we're going to need a lot of bullets.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat4_c: {0.07->2.00}Now that they know they're losing,{2.04->5.81}the Cyborgs are scrambling to build more Megafactories all over.{7.03->8.00}Desperate.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat4_d: {0.00->0.32}Well,{1.02->4.69}if they had just realized that their ideology was inferior sooner,{5.26->7.29}they could have saved themselves a lot of bombings.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat5_a: {0.02->6.71}They may cling to Cyberstan for now, but forever? Heh, that's statistically improbable.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat5_c: {0.00->3.22}We must focus on defending Super Earth, Helldiver.{3.82->4.76}She needs us.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_beat5_d: {0.00->3.56}The Cyborgs have always dreamed of destroying Super Earth.{3.94->8.87}Well, that dream ends now, with the scheduled wake-up call of justice.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_arc6_service_: {0.04->5.39}A Helldiver needs a strong, solid ship crew to be able to deliver maximum devastation.{5.94->8.65}What would we have done without our Service Tech, eh?{9.02->11.36}For longer than we've already been without her, I mean.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_fully_upgrade: {0.00->4.33}Nothing beats spreading Democracy in a fully-upgraded Super Destroyer.{4.79->5.95}Well done, Helldiver.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_ei: {0.00->2.30}I'm not sure what to make of these Automatons. {2.73->6.91}I'm sure they're no match for the Helldivers, but that's no reason for complacency.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_fi: {0.00->0.65}Helldiver - {1.10->6.47}successful missions are rewarded by Super Earth High Command. These resources can be used to make {6.47->8.49}your Destroyer even more powerful.

{0.14->0.57}Oh, {1.03->5.72}I see you've secured some research samples. You can spend those at the terminal over there to {5.74->7.20}upgrade the Super Destroyer. {7.77->8.52}Get enough of them, {8.88->11.22}and we'll see what this baby can really do.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_fo: {0.02->4.23}This is the largest Helldiver mobilization since the First Galactic War. {4.71->6.63}Plenty of allies we can join up with.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_ni: {0.00->4.30}This is the largest Helldiver mobilization we've seen since the First Galactic War. {4.73->6.79}Our enemies don't know what's coming.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_se: {0.04->1.51}More samples. Good. {1.98->5.04}I'm looking forward to seeing what Destroyer upgrades you can get for them.

{0.00->2.52}The Terminids might be escaped farm animals, {3.86->4.11}but {4.96->5.92}they've been multiplying {6.62->8.45}faster than our regular units {9.23->9.77}can keep up {9.77->10.00}with. But now that the Helldivers are going in, we'll have them under control soon enough.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_si: {0.00->4.53}We're fighting three fronts: the Terminids on one side, the Automatons on the other...{4.91->7.46}and the Illuminate, who seem to be everywhere.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_te: {0.00->4.92}Even though the First Galactic War ended nearly a hundred years ago now, Managed Democracy has {4.92->7.95}stayed vigilant. Exactly for developments like these.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding_th: {0.00->1.28}The bridge is fore of here. {1.64->4.21}Talk to the Democracy Officer when you're ready to join the fight.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding01_: {0.00->0.36}Me? {1.01->5.68}I'm the Shipmaster of this Super Destroyer. I make sure you have everything you need to spread {5.68->7.52}Managed Democracy to our enemies.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_onboarding02_: {0.00->1.63}Feel free to take a look around. {2.05->3.41}This is your ship, after all.
vo_hub_shipmaster_interact_worldbuilding: {0.00->2.02}Here's the problem with the bots, Helldiver: {2.39->4.57}Their whole society revolves around violence. {5.01->6.43}If they actually won the war, {6.82->8.31}they'd have no idea how to function.
{0.00->4.50}The bots are proof that if you put enough 1s and 0s together, you can threaten the entire galaxy.
{0.00->2.46}01000001 {2.74->5.21}01001000. {5.60->7.66}That was the first intercepted bot message. {8.12->10.82}Our analysts think it means "Kill all humans."
{0.00->5.18}The bots are simple enough. Little more than bits of metal welded to a gun and a CPU, with a single {5.18->6.32}overriding directive: {7.01->7.64}kill.
{0.00->4.63}You know, the problem with the bugs is that they're relentless expansionists. In their region of {4.63->7.50}space, we've found them on nearly every planet we've settled.
{0.00->2.24}If we don't stop the bugs' mindless reproduction, {2.60->4.33}the burden's going to fall on our children. {4.78->6.28}And our children's children.
{0.00->2.38}The bugs are basically an out-of-control pest. {2.90->4.18}And you're the exterminator.
{0.00->3.57}If the Terminids had stayed on the Element 710 farms where they belonged, {4.16->5.95}all this bloodshed could've been avoided.
{0.00->4.57}The DSS is so large, it's run by 10 Ship Masters.{5.00->5.95}Can you imagine?
{0.07->3.45}The logistics involved in running the DSS...{4.31->6.68}I could think about it all day.
{0.00->1.64}Ammo, fuel, personnelâ{2.07->4.92}I manage everything on this ship with one thing in mind: {5.37->8.12}making sure you have the support you need, Helldiver.
{0.00->2.39}I haven't set foot on Super Earth myself, but {2.88->5.24}I've heard it's even more spectacular in person.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_city_a: {0.64->4.88}The fewer cities we control, the harder the planet will be to defend.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_city_b: {0.00->2.64}We can't let the enemy take our cities from us.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_city_c: {0.00->3.24}We have to get back down there. The city needs us.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_comman: {0.00->1.85}We knew the odds were against us.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_cybers: {0.00->2.87}We'll show those traitors what vengeance looks like.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_factor: {4.16->5.70}That Cyborg Megafactory is still standing... but not for long.
{0.00->3.52}We WILL level these factories, whatever it takes.
{0.00->3.92}For every fallen hero, we'll bomb another ten repair shops.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_failure_hive_a: {0.00->2.00}The Terminid Hives are death traps.{2.17->4.44}That's why we only send the best.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_accide: {0.00->1.49}Lots of friendly fire. {1.85->4.43}Unfortunate, but undoubtedly necessary.
{0.00->4.50}0 friendly fire incidents is the sort of statistical anomaly I welcome.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bot_a: {0.00->1.72}A lot of dead bots down there. {2.41->3.18}Good job.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bot_b: {0.00->3.12}I don't know if those Automatons were ever technically alive, {3.49->5.24}but they're definitely dead now.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bot_c: {0.00->2.18}A few more bots out of commission.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bot_d: {0.00->2.64}Keep it up, and pretty soon there won't be any bots left. {3.14->4.69}Just Managed Democracy.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bot_f: {0.00->2.38}Those bots didn't stand a chance against you.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bug_a: {0.00->0.96}Welcome back. {1.32->4.37}The Terminids you defeated will soon fuel the war effort.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_bug_d: {0.00->2.56}That should help keep the bug population in check.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_city_a: {0.00->5.59}The collateral damage you did to the city was well worth its defense. Not to mention, unavoidable.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_city_b: {0.00->1.02}Excellent work.{1.47->4.74}If I didn't know you'd never received urban warfare training,{5.33->6.76}I'd never have guessed.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_city_c: {0.00->4.24}Welcome back. That city is one step closer to Freedom.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_city_d: {0.00->1.13}Good work down there.{1.52->4.34}They'll regret trespassing into our cities.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_comman: {0.00->1.73}I knew you had it in you!
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_cybers: {0.00->2.95}Their form of government is no match for ours!
{0.03->2.89}Soon, the Cyborgs'll be back in their mines!
{0.00->1.76}Did you spit on Cyberstan for me?
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_cyborg: {1.23->5.33}One less Megafactory. A win for the human gene pool.
vo_hub_shipmaster_mission_success_deaths: {0.00->2.64}Every Helldiver lost is an unspeakable tragedy, {3.23->6.62}but this mission had fewer unspeakable tragedies than average.
{0.00->1.32}Welcome back, Helldivers. {1.93->7.10}Your comrades who didn't return will rest easy knowing that this mission had a lower than average {7.10->7.89}casualty rate.
{0.00->2.59}It's a good day when we lose so few Helldivers.
{0.04->4.00}This mission required fewer heroes to make the ultimate sacrifice than normal.

vo_hub_shipmaster_passive_arc6_beat2_a: {0.00->1.40}Look at what they've done to this planet...{1.77->7.54}Exploiting every inch of it, leeching its oil, destroying the local flora to build their ugly{7.54->8.52}infrastructure...{9.45->10.48}Makes me sick.
vo_hub_shipmaster_passive_arc6_beat2_b: {0.00->2.03}Cyberstan used to be a good planet, you know?{2.48->4.45}Wasn't called "Cyberstan" back then, of course.

vo_hub_shipmaster_passive_arc6_beat5_b: {0.06->4.02}So the Cyborgs just reproduce and reproduce, like rats...{4.58->7.12}spitting in the face of family values.

vo_hub_shipmaster_react_onboarding_premi: {0.03->4.20}If you've got any Medals or Super Credits, you can spend them in the Acquisition Center. {4.72->6.64}That's where they sell the fancy stuff.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_a: {0.05->2.95}Flags in rags, we're advancing on Cyberstan...{2.95->5.91}just like the heroes of the first Galactic War.{5.91->8.56}This'll be something to tell the grandkids!
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_b: {0.09->5.59}Cyborgs... I can't believe they used to be real people, real citizens. And{6.19->8.29}they survived all this time... what,{8.62->12.30}sustained only by oil, and hatred for the electoral process.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_c: {0.14->1.84}So what, the Cyborgs are back?{2.45->3.74}Big deal! {4.27->5.53}We always knew this day would come. {6.24->8.97}The Service Technician forums have predicted this for like,{9.49->10.51}eighty years already.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_d: {0.04->2.21}Sometimes I wish it was me out there,{2.75->5.68} driving my own Bastion Tank into the heart of Cyberstan. {7.14->9.73}But then I repeat my Assignment Gratitude Mantra, {9.73->10.43}and bottle those{10.43->11.11}selfish wishes{11.11->11.67} right up.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_e: {0.04->6.18}I am grateful for my assignment. My tasks suit me and I suit them.{6.18->12.32}A menial task is a glorious task. My ship needs me, and I need it.{12.32->15.25}I am grateful for my assignment.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat1_f: {0.05->3.73}Hey-hey, if you get a tank, crush some bots for me, yeah?
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_a: {0.06->5.48}So, this is it! Cyberstan, the Den of Dissidence.{5.48->11.34}Doesn't look much like the cartoons, huh? Less spikes?
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_b: {0.08->4.92}So the Cyborgs are back, after all those years of peaceful service to Managed Democracy. {5.76->9.66}And they'd rather die on the battlefield than live under Super Earth?{10.26->11.21}That's stupid.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_c: {0.09->3.61}Did you know that Cyborgs don't have hearts? It's true!{3.61->10.92}And no brains either, apparentlyâthat must be how they were able to betray Managed Democracy a hundred years ago.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_d: {0.04->5.18}The Cyborgs must be so full of themselves, thinking that whatever they come up with is better than{5.18->6.16}Super Earth Freedom.{6.71->7.25}Please.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_e: {0.03->2.47}I mean, I can't even imagine trying to secede.{2.70->6.09}If you're not in any state databases, do you even exist?
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat2_f: {0.01->4.53}You'd think one hundred years of rehabilitative labor would have changed their tune.{5.10->7.43}But I guess some people are just too depraved... {8.64->11.70}welp, that's why they're not people anymore.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat3_a: {0.00->1.04}It all makes sense.{1.40->4.95}You know, the Cyborgs were doing rehabilitative labor for a hundred years,{4.98->8.89}so they realized what a boon non-compensated labor can be to a society.{8.89->11.16} So they tried to build their own laborers.{11.24->14.27}But since they didn't base the Automatons on Super Earth values, {14.27->16.73}the Bots just ended up as mindless war-slaves.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat3_b: {0.03->5.04}The Cyborgs sent the Automatons, their mindless war-slaves, off-world in secret.{5.48->9.84}Then the Automatons chose to come back to set their masters free, since they aren't sentient{9.84->12.35}enough to even realize that they're slaves to begin with.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat3_c: {0.07->3.57}The Bots are only attacking Super Earth to distract us from Cyberstan.{4.22->6.42} It's petty payback, is what it is, {7.09->9.65}going after our home just because we're reclaiming theirs.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_a: {0.08->1.47}Cyberstan is ours!{1.98->2.97}Can you believe it? {3.46->6.35}Yes you can, because we're the ones who took it!
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_b: {0.05->2.74}That's right, that's OUR planet!{2.74->6.44}OUR planet, and don't those Bots forget it!{6.44->9.38}Whoo, I might need to have a seat.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_c: {0.09->5.54}I never doubted you for a second, Helldiver! I knew you'd rout the Cyborgs from Cyberstan. {6.13->9.93}I can't wait to see the look on their treacherous faces when we put them right back,{10.40->12.06}in even deeper mines this time!
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_d: {0.00->3.40}I don't really want to know what those Cyborgs are up to in those Megafactories. {4.07->6.83}It's wrong. And it's icky.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_e: {0.07->5.90}The Cyborgs just keep mass-reproducing, through those Megafactories that they're building everywhere.{5.90->8.52}Isn't it gross, how they multiply?{9.22->12.08}They're not even starting families, they're just producing more soldiers.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat4_f: {0.05->4.34}I heard that Cyborgs don't even use any permits to determine who should reproduce.{6.36->8.23}It's like they don't care about their gene pool at all.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat5_a: {0.00->5.23}We have to protect Super Earth! If we don't protect our home, we might never get to see it for{5.23->5.87}ourselves.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat5_b: {0.03->3.61}I always dreamed of watching the Moon from Super Earth...{3.61->7.57}maybe even take part in a test firing, put my own crater on it.{7.57->11.90}That was before I took the Workplace Assignment Assessment, of course.
vo_hub_technician_interact_arc6_beat5_c: {0.00->1.87}We'll get Cyberstan back.{2.60->5.78}The Cyborgs will learn what happens when you refuse to submit to Freedom.
vo_hub_technician_interact_bastion_a: {0.04->3.47}I can't believe Bastion Tanks are back in production!{4.43->6.32}Imagine if kid me had known about it,{6.32->9.74}back when she was collecting all the Armored Fighting Vehicles from cereal boxes.{10.79->11.54}She would have freaked!
vo_hub_technician_interact_bastion_b: {0.00->2.61}Did you know that most tank production facilities {2.61->5.07}were turned into theme parks after the First Galactic War?{5.77->8.17}My parents used to take me to this fairytale-themed one.{8.59->13.40}The chimney towers were painted pink, and they said Lady Liberty lived there, and the Helldivers{13.40->15.47}all protected her from the evil Dissident King.
vo_hub_technician_interact_bastion_c: {0.63->5.57}Now, all those old theme parks have been re-converted back into tank factories. The fairy towers{5.57->6.56}are chimneys again,{6.96->9.14}huffing and puffing in service to the war machine.{9.80->11.38}All thanks to you, Helldiver!
vo_hub_technician_interact_bastion_d: {0.07->5.75}Hey Helldiver, did you hear? Tanks are back in production! Real tanks!
vo_hub_technician_interact_bastion_e: {0.04->4.31}Imagine the sound of tank treads crushing the skulls of our enemies.{4.31->6.72}I like to think it sounds like popcorn.
vo_hub_technician_interact_generic_d: {0.00->2.63}You truly are one of Liberty's greatest heroes.
vo_hub_technician_interact_generic_e: {0.04->5.27}If Terminids didn't decompose into Element 710, we'd have eradicated them completely a long time {5.27->5.61}ago. {7.05->7.25}They should count themselves lucky.
vo_hub_technician_interact_generic_f: {0.00->1.54}Ship's in top shape, Helldiver. {1.94->4.35}Ready to dispense Democracy wherever you choose.
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_a: {0.00->0.78}Grateful to be back.{1.40->2.95}And to have been gone!{3.38->6.76}You learn a lot in Professional Development Training, like{7.14->8.72}how to create better documentation,{9.26->9.59}and{10.47->12.88}how to stay focused on a projector screen for hours at a time.
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_b: {0.04->3.37}I'm so sorry you had to be without a Service Tech for so long!{3.37->12.68}I mean, here I get to enjoy Professional Development Training for months and months, while you guys are stuck here without anyone to un-jam the Nutritious Meal Dispensers.{12.68->15.04}I hope you still got your allotted calories!
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_c: {0.00->1.10}I'm sure you noticed the{1.69->4.26}drop in efficiency among the support crew while I was gone.{4.98->6.61}I hope the Democracy Officer wasn't{6.98->7.69}too hard on them?
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_d: {0.02->3.32}Man, Professional Development Training sure is{4.17->5.03}something. {5.79->10.24}And all because some Service Technicians asked too many questions about that one strike...{11.08->13.66}But hey, at least you make a lot of friends strapped into those chairs!
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_e: {0.00->4.48}Apparently, non-compensated labor was what that strike was about, the one that{4.48->10.72}some other Service Technicians got a little too interested in. But hey, we all scored some{10.72->15.77}Professional Development Training, the bad apples got re-educated, so it all worked out!
vo_hub_technician_interact_return_f: {0.05->7.97}Isn't it great that when even just a couple of people from the same corps shows signs of dissidency, all of us get extra Professional Development Training?{7.97->9.50}High Command truly does care.
vo_hub_technician_interact_worldbuilding: {0.00->4.58}Helldiver! Every day, you put yourself at risk to defend freedom and liberty, and I just wanna say... {5.35->5.80}Thank you.
{0.00->2.82}Nobody back home can believe I get to talk to Helldivers every day.
{0.00->3.36}I don't blame themâall of us had Helldiver posters all over our bedrooms growin' up.
{0.43->2.18}I still have to pinch myself sometimes.
{0.00->3.41}You ever run into any equipment issues, let me know. I've got your back.
{0.00->4.95}Before I joined the service, I'd never left my home planet. Now I see a new planet almost every day.
{0.03->3.07}Makes me appreciate what a beautiful Democracy it'll be{3.07->5.32}once we finish stamping out those Fascist bugs.
{0.00->2.56}When I was a kid, I always loved All Heroes' Eve.
{0.00->4.16}The adults would all dress up as Bugs, and ring our doorbells and we'd have to fire 3 shots in the {4.16->6.33}air and say "No Fascism here, insects!"
{0.00->3.70}At the end of the night, they'd re-enact the Battle of Liberty Peak, and we'd all get cake.
{0.62->1.99}I can't wait to get home again. It's {2.50->3.90}the little things you miss, you know?
{0.00->4.23}Like sitting in candlelight on Heroes' Day, chanting with my family, and renewing our vows of {4.23->5.30}Citizenship together.
{0.00->3.50}Back when I was a kid, the local Loyalty Officer would give us sweets for telling him what was {3.50->4.95}goin' on with the adults around town.
{0.00->2.15}Things they were sayin', where they were headed, that sort of stuff.
{0.00->2.55}Nice to look back and know you were helping keep everyone safe, {2.97->3.94}even if you didn't know it.
{0.00->4.04}Hey there, Helldiver. I know you put your neck on the line for regular folks like me every day.
{0.00->1.61}It's not much, but I donate {2.12->5.48}15% of every one of my paychecks to the War Fund. It's the least I can do.
{0.00->2.21}I heard the chow hall got a shipment of Purples in today.
{0.06->3.53}They're not my favorite fruit, personally, but it's nice just to have something different.
{0.00->4.11}Democracy is kind of like these machines I work on. It needs regular maintenance to keep running {4.11->4.60}smoothly.
{0.07->3.51}And the Bugs are sort of like if...bugs got into one of these machines.{3.51->5.90}The only thing to do would be to exterminate them.
{0.02->4.42}If I had all the Super Credits in the galaxy, I'd try to find some way to convince our enemies to {4.42->5.20}embrace Freedom.
{0.00->4.75}Maybe print a trillion pamphlets, or build a billion interplanetary radio stations, something to {4.75->6.64}break through whatever propaganda they're feedin' em.
{0.02->2.99}*sigh* But that's just a pipe dream. We gotta kill 'em all.
{0.00->3.58}Whenever the war gets me down, I try to remember that at least I'm a free citizen.{4.01->5.50}It's up to me how I serve Super Earth.
{0.00->0.95}Can't take that for granted.
{0.00->3.15}When I need some motivation, I like to read The Helldiver Ethos. {3.63->4.45}Picks me right up.
{0.00->3.89}Sometimes I read too much and get overly motivated and kinda jittery and need to put it down.
{0.05->3.95}I heard that some dissidents wanted us to select our own candidates instead of using the algorithm.
{0.46->4.14}*scoffs* Great idea. Everyone'll just become a political expert overnight. {4.51->5.53}Treasonous morons.
{0.00->5.21}I remember when I was a kid, they sold stickers in the Freedom Catalogue that were these little squished Bugs.
{0.00->3.66}You put 'em on the bottom of your boot, and it was like you were squashin' bugs every time you took {3.66->3.95}a step.
{0.00->3.95}Everyone was collectin' 'em, tryin' to get the rare ones. Man, those things were cool.
{0.00->3.98}I don't know how you do it, Helldiver. Facing off against monsters who haven't got a democratic {3.98->4.86}bone in their body.
{0.00->1.11}If they even have bones.
{0.00->4.09}You know, if it weren't for Super Earth, I couldn't even afford these high-tech tools I use every day.
{0.00->1.74}They lease 'em out to me, so I can do my job.
{0.00->1.13}Pretty low interest rate, too.
{0.00->2.75}Super Earth really is the best place to live in the entire galaxy, isn't it?
{0.00->1.43}Sometimes, when I can't sleep, I {1.82->3.48}imagine what it would be like to live there.
{0.00->1.88}Long ship deployments are great for savin' up.
{0.00->3.40}You get the family separation bonus, which more than makes up for the bunk and chow fees.
{0.05->4.08}Course, I don't qualify for the bonus since I haven't been approved yet. But once I am, I'll have a {4.08->5.16}nice nest egg saved up.
{0.03->3.09}If I were a Helldiver, I think my favorite enemy to kill would be the Bugs.
{0.13->2.97}It's gotta be real satisfyin', hearin' 'em crunch under your boot.
{0.03->3.68}If I were a Helldiver, I think my favorite enemy to kill would be the Bots.
{0.00->3.93}I bet watchin' 'em spark and explode really lights up your Freedom-lovin' heart.
{0.00->2.95}I just set all the ship's clocks back an hour for Freedom Savings Time.
{0.00->2.95}I'm gonna spend my extra hour doin' another round of maintenance checks.
{0.03->2.53}It's nice, cuz today I won't have to work overtime to get 'em done.
{0.04->2.68}I heard dissidents set another weapons factory on fire.
{0.10->3.64}They didn't take responsibility, of course. Tried to blame it on "working conditions."
{0.00->2.70}Tell that to the 27 dead patriots and their families.
{0.00->2.95}Back when I was a kid, we used to play "Bug Killers" out in the schoolyard.
{0.00->2.95}Whoever could kill the most bugs by the end of recess won.
{0.00->1.58}Sometimes the teachers would join in, too.
{0.20->2.02}I earned a few more Citizenship points the other day.
{0.00->3.15}All it took was reportin' some unpatriotic talk I heard in the barracks.
{0.00->3.25}Now there's one less dissident, and I get one more doctor's visit every year.
{0.03->1.61}It's fish fry night at the chow hall. {2.50->4.11}I don't know what planets they get these fish from, {4.11->6.71}and I don't care. It's all fried, and it's all good.
{0.04->3.04}When I face Super Earth for the Vow of Allegiance every morning,{3.04->5.05}I can almost always feel where it is. {5.50->7.35}Even in a brand-new part of the galaxy.
{0.07->2.36}Sometimes, I almost feel bad for our enemies, {2.86->4.09}livin' without Democracy. {4.93->7.76}No say over their own lives, just believin' whatever they're told. {8.64->9.19}It's sad.
{0.00->2.78}I'm hoping to raise my Citizenship level by the time I retire.{3.15->6.25}If I ever have kids, I'd want them to be able to purchase a small pet someday.
{0.00->0.87}Like a goldfish, {1.62->2.57}or even a hamster.
{0.00->2.95}As much as I hate the Bugs, I hate dissidents even more.
{0.00->3.04}They were handed freedom, and chose to spit in its face. {3.50->4.71}Death is too good for them.
{0.00->4.75}You know, sometimes as a technician, you gotta realize when something's beyond repair.

vo_tutorial_mod01_sec01_bot_stim_thanks_: {0.02->5.91}You saved my life, fellow human soldier. You displayed uncommon grace under pressure.
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec01_instructor_movem: {0.06->4.61}In all my years, I've never witnessed such acrobatic perfection. {5.13->9.62}I think you're ready for our highly realistic battlefield simulator. {10.01->10.75}That's rightâ{11.16->12.38}the real deal.

vo_tutorial_mod01_sec01_instructor_trenc: {0.06->8.20}Impeccable! Like I always say, the best simulation is a real simulation. Now, activate the {8.20->10.34}battlefield injury simulator!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec01_instructor_welco: {0.00->2.32}Atten-hut! {2.32->4.39}I'm General Brasch{4.39->8.11}âSuper Earth's oldest and most grizzled war hero, {8.11->11.19}and I'm not easy to impress. {11.19->15.32}Helldiver Training is the toughest test in the galaxy{15.32->18.54}âbut I can tell that doesn't scare you.{18.54->20.31}Impressive!

vo_tutorial_mod01_sec02_instructor_explo: {0.00->2.02}What a catapult of an arm! {2.53->4.26}Onto the next section!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec02_instructor_reinf: {0.06->1.66}Well done, soldier. {2.02->7.42}It takes uncommon resolve to move past the death of your ally with so little pause.
{0.05->7.33}What a fine soldier. Your brother-in-arms. A battle-forged bond that could never be replaced.
{0.00->0.81}Next lesson: {1.19->5.16}Helldivers fight in squads. Meet your new squadmate!

{0.00->7.47}Man down! Don't sweat it, soldier: friendly fire is just an unavoidable fact of life. Nothin' AT {7.47->13.75}ALL you can do to prevent it! What you CAN do is use the Reinforcement Stratagem to replace your {13.75->14.76}fallen comrade!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec02_instructor_sidea: {0.00->7.73}Deadeye precision! Now, use a grenade to close up that Bug Hole! Otherwise, they'll just keep {7.76->8.30}comin'.
{0.11->9.59}There it is. A Terminid. The Scourge of Liberty. Arm yourself and show that Bug the true meaning of {9.59->10.38}Freedom.
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec02_instructor_strat: {0.08->1.85}Look up in the sky, recruit; {2.47->4.78}that there is a Super Destroyer, {5.29->9.27}and its all-powerful Stratagems are yours to command.
{0.00->3.30}Use a Stratagem now to take care of those bugs. {4.00->6.84}Just make sure you take a few steps back.
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec03_instructor_loado: {0.10->6.79}Load yourself up, recruit. This is the real deal. It's just you, the gun in your hands, {6.79->11.21}and 2,000 megatons of explosives in close orbit. That's it!
{0.00->7.22}Piece of advice: movin' around makes it hard to aim. For A-list accuracy, stay still while you {7.22->7.66}shoot!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec03_instructor_missi: {0.06->6.63}Glorious work, recruit.  Already you've advanced the cause of democracy farther than any soldier {6.63->7.73}I've ever seen. {8.15->12.93}You've proved your worth. You're ready to don your cape.
{0.00->5.49}This area is in dire need of liberation from the Terminid scourge.{5.49->7.03}Activate that terminal!
{0.00->2.69}That's the flag of Super Earth, recruit! {3.08->4.89}Plant it in the designated area.
{0.09->6.49}Those unthinkin' bugs never stood a chance against a genius tactical mind like yourself. {6.97->9.72}You are literally invincible!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec03_instructor_resup: {0.11->7.69}Hold up, recruit. It's finally time to call in the big guns. Use the support weapon stratagem to {7.69->9.69}get yourself a real weapon.
{0.05->9.23}Load up, soldier, because playtime's over. You've reached your final test. THISâis the REAL DEAL. {9.57->10.35}Get to it!
{0.03->6.16}That's the stuff! Now, use a Resupply Stratagem to get yourself some more ammo!
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec04_instructor_caper: {0.07->2.53}Now THERE'S a Helldiver. {2.90->5.28}You remind me of a younger me{5.47->9.63}âfearless, invulnerable, and 7 feet tall.
{0.13->2.59}Step up to the platform, recruit. {3.51->5.06}You've earned it.
vo_tutorial_mod01_sec04_instructor_cryor: {0.08->3.55}You're finally ready for the realest deal of 'em all: {3.95->5.72}The Galactic War. {6.17->9.73}Liberty. Democracy. Justice. {10.20->12.34}They're in your hands now.  {12.74->15.82}General Brasch, over and out.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_a: {0.22->1.43}Focus on your breath. {3.36->4.91}Inhale Democracy, {6.60->8.64}exhale treasonous thoughts. {11.05->12.25}Inhale Liberty, exhale flesh-warping, brain-killing Illuminate mind control.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_ab: {0.09->9.97}Can you smell it? The tantalizing scent of calamari, frying.{9.97->23.41}Whenever you see Illuminate writing, recall the scent, and refocus on your mission.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_b: {0.15->1.69}Visualize the Flag. {3.67->4.24}See it {5.08->6.64}wrapping around your legs, {8.10->8.93}your arms, {10.50->11.28}your body, {13.04->14.00}your face. {16.08->17.29}You are safe.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_bb: {0.00->4.93}Remember: a patriotic mind is a protected mind.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_c: {0.16->2.40}Hold a Stratagem Beacon. {4.36->7.83}Sleep with a Stratagem Beacon in your hand. {10.00->11.96}Let the Stratagem Beacon {12.86->14.75}become an extension of your body, like your hand or your gun.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_d: {8.83->9.31}Look {9.68->10.41}around. Everyone {10.97->12.27}on this ship depends on you. Let that pressure turn you into the diamond Super Earth needs.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_db: {0.01->2.27}The Galaxy embraces us.{2.27->11.98}When its nonhuman inhabitants try to murder you on sight, they are a disturbance to the natural order.{11.98->13.87}Super Earth's order.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_e: {0.42->5.49}Relax. Feel the endless void of space.{5.49->17.31}Feel the cold, vast loneliness pressing against the hull of your ship. Feel yourself fall.{17.31->21.34}Now, let Super Earth catch you.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_eb: {0.00->2.96}Ever marvelled at Illuminate technology?{4.03->6.71}Report to your Loyalty Officer.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_f: {0.06->2.06}Make a fist with your gun-wielding {2.06->2.70}hand.{2.70->4.11} Clench it.{4.11->5.01} Relax.{5.01->11.15} Now, hold your fist up in the Helldiver salute.{11.15->15.38} This fist is holding our future.{15.38->23.96} Clench it as hard as you can, for as long as your body can manage.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_fb: {0.03->5.73}Remember: There is no Enlightenment outside Democracy's light.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_g: {0.15->0.87}Envision the {0.87->1.74}future.{1.74->5.09} Your descendants, smiling.{5.09->10.06} Super Earth, bright-towered in the sun.{10.06->21.99} Your cape, mounted on a pole, waving in the gentle breeze -- in time with the Super Earth flag.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_gb: {0.00->8.65}Remember: our nation's founders would be proud of you. You are someone they would LOVE to grab a{8.73->9.48}beer with.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_h: {0.07->1.73}Listen to your heart, {2.66->4.77}the strength of its beat. {6.14->8.59}Is that the strength of one {8.59->9.00}person? No. It is the strength of all humanity. Organic, endoskeletal humanity.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_i: {0.00->2.09}When all is quiet, {3.25->5.22}listen for the Super Earth {5.38->6.15}anthem.{6.15->8.67} Hear it in your heart.{8.67->11.41} Sing it with your mouth.{11.41->17.66} Belt it from your diaphragm, for all the Galaxy to hear.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_j: {13.19->14.46}Close your eyes. {15.47->16.34}See nothing. {16.34->16.81}Be nothing.{16.81->18.50} This is life without the Right to Vote.{18.50->19.39} Now, open your eyes.{19.39->22.48} See the world as a Registered Voter -- resplendent in Democracy's light.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_k: {0.07->2.23}Close your eyes.{2.23->10.37}See yourself standing before a great nebula of Terminid spores.{10.37->18.13}Clench your fist. Send your fist into that tyrannical cloud.{18.13->25.76}Hear the enemy scream. Watch as they suffer. You are whole.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_l: {0.00->9.83}You are here. You are home. You are aboard your very own Super Destroyer.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_m: {0.02->8.52}Remember: as long as you fight for Super Earth - you matter.{8.52->15.34}If you stop, you don't. But you are - and you do.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_n: {0.00->0.86}Remember:{1.46->4.17}your enemies can never touch you,{4.70->8.24}as long as Democracy is in your heart.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_o: {0.04->5.21}Trust this: your body is a weapon.{5.21->10.81}Trust this: your mind is a fortress.{10.81->16.13}Trust this: your soul is a pure lake.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_p: {0.00->3.49}Who can say, when the first star was lit?{4.18->14.19}What the essence of Democracy is? When Space is truly Free? We can.{14.19->16.75}Your government can.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_q: {0.00->8.81}Steel your heart. Let nothing in. You are empty; you are numb;{10.21->12.07}you are at peace.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_r: {0.00->2.60}Running water. Do you hear{2.60->17.93}it? There - a flowing river of E-710. Hear the river growing, larger and larger. Now, its a rushing{17.96->18.69}torrent.{19.29->24.37}It overflows its banks, washing away all opposition{24.77->28.63}and flooding the Galaxy with prosperity.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_s: {0.06->8.93}Close your eyes. Picture yourself within the Hellpod. It is a mother's womb.{8.93->14.92}Inside, you are safe. You have everything you need.{14.92->19.52}You are on the way to where you belong.{19.52->24.82}You will arrive exactly when you are meant to.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_t: {0.00->9.58}A reading from the Citizens' Oath, passage 8:28: We trust that in all things,{10.00->13.79}the Government works for the those who work for it.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_u: {0.00->6.21}Take a moment to mourn. Mourn the fallen. Mourn{6.26->15.94}those twisted into Voteless abominations. Mourn those juiced and slurrified by the Automatons.{17.13->18.43}Now breathe,{19.12->19.90}fast,{20.42->21.14}faster,{21.67->27.06}hyperventilate until that grief turns to righteous rage.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_v: {0.00->5.06}Remember: No matter your age or experience,{5.70->8.70}no matter how long you have served,{9.32->13.96}and no matter your actual contributions on the battlefield:{15.10->18.52}you helped save Super Earth.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_x: {0.09->5.73}Stretch your body. Stretch your mind.{5.73->13.61}Let it wander, let it wonder, about Liberty's grace.{13.61->24.16}This is where curiosity is safe; when dwelling on what we already know.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_y: {0.00->7.68}Remember: Those ready to sacrifice everything can never be truly harmed.
vo_tv_meditation_generic_z: {0.05->12.53}Relax. Float, as if in a hydropod. Now, envision a squid, swimming toward you.{12.53->26.76}Reaching out its tentacles. Now take out your blade, and lop off every single, slimy arm.{26.76->28.81}You are safe.

vo_tv_meditation_remember_b: {0.00->1.78}Take a moment to remember: {3.00->4.77}You are a hero.
vo_tv_meditation_remember_c: {0.03->1.72}Take a moment to remember: {3.76->5.90}Every sacrifice matters.
vo_tv_meditation_remember_d: {0.00->1.77}Take a moment to remember: {2.86->5.12}Super Earth loves you.
vo_tv_meditation_remember_e: {0.03->1.95}Take a moment to remember: {3.12->4.74}Hugs are free.
vo_tv_meditation_remember_f: {0.05->1.89}Take a moment to remember: {3.04->5.69}Every Helldiver is your friend. {7.02->8.69}Every single one.
